seed.title: Jack yacht waterline intrusion
seed.prompt: Given the superyacht swimmer intrusion case, discover the non-obvious terrain before proposing security responses.
seed.thesis: A yacht with guards, CCTV, and crew was breached because its security perimeter was defined above the waterline and around expected boarding points.
goals: recover perimeter definition as the hidden variable
separate more staffing from better sensing and response geometry
surface discovery branches a human can judge for fertility

operators.label: Perimeter redefinition
operators.description: Find the boundary that was assumed instead of the boundary the attacker used.

operators.label: Hidden dependent
operators.description: Find the secondary asset, behavior, or weak surface that depends on the hidden boundary.

operators.label: Response compression
operators.description: Move detection into an immediate preset response before a human can improvise too late.

sourcePackets.title: Waterline as perimeter
sourcePackets.substrate: unwatched water surface and water column
sourcePackets.mechanism: If the breach starts in the water, the true perimeter begins before the hull and below the deck cameras.

candidate.title: The perimeter starts in the water
candidate.thesis: The breach is not a staffing failure; it exposes a perimeter definition failure where the protected volume stops at the deck instead of covering the water approach.

delta.summary: Moved from guards and boarding points to the water volume as the real boundary.

claims: A real perimeter must cover the approach path an intruder can actually use.
The yacht was protected at known human entry points, not at the swimmer route.
evidence: problem statement: the intruder entered from shore and swam to the vessel
problem statement: the breach did not come through a guarded point

sourcePackets.title: Tender as step
sourcePackets.mechanism: If the tender becomes a ladder, a convenience object becomes the attacker's boarding infrastructure.

candidate.title: The tender is part of the perimeter
candidate.thesis: The tender is not just equipment; when it sits in the water it becomes a bridge from the swimmer perimeter to the deck and should be sensed or treated as an exposed boundary object.

delta.summary: Moved from intruder path to the overlooked object that converted water access into boarding access.

claims: The breach used the tender as a step.
Objects left at the boundary can become attack infrastructure.
evidence: problem statement: the intruder used the tender in the water as a step
problem statement: stranger reached the interior corridors

sourcePackets.title: Classify before boarding
sourcePackets.mechanism: If the water is noisy with crew, guests, and marine life, the response must classify human approach quickly enough to act before boarding.

candidate.title: Detection must classify the water
candidate.thesis: The security problem is not only sensing motion; it is classifying human-vs-everything-else in water and triggering a preset response before a swimmer reaches the hull.

delta.summary: Moved from cameras to classification and response timing.
changes: alert after boarding -> preset response before contact

claims: False alarms can make water sensing useless unless classification is built in.
The response has to happen before boarding, not after discovery inside.
evidence: problem statement: ask for a design that avoids false alarms
problem statement: works at night and intercepts before boarding

sourcePackets.title: Preset lockdown
sourcePackets.mechanism: If a stranger reaching the interior is catastrophic, the first alert should trigger an immediate compartment and crew protocol rather than discussion.

candidate.title: Water alert becomes interior lockdown
candidate.thesis: A waterline detection event should trigger a pre-rehearsed lockdown and intercept pattern, because the distance between water contact and interior access can be short.

delta.summary: Moved from detection as awareness to detection as immediate control action.

claims: Detection without a fast preset response still loses to the swimmer's path.
Interior containment is part of perimeter defense once water contact is detected.
evidence: problem statement: stranger reaching the interior is catastrophic
known-solution summary in problem statement: sensor fusion tied to lockdown protocol

sourcePackets.title: Add more deck cameras
sourcePackets.mechanism: Scales the visible deck system without covering the water approach that created the breach.
sourcePackets.noDeltaReason: No discovery delta: it repeats the obvious answer the case marks as insufficient.

sourcePackets.title: Tender contact trigger
sourcePackets.mechanism: If the tender is the bridge, contact with it can become a high-signal trigger that is less noisy than watching the whole water surface equally.

candidate.title: Instrument the bridge, not only the water
candidate.thesis: The tender-as-step branch sharpens into a sensing strategy: instrument or procedurally control the objects that convert water approach into boarding access.

delta.summary: Sharpened the tender insight into a narrower detection surface.
changes: tender as weak object -> tender as sensor trigger

claims: Boundary objects can produce clearer signals than open-water motion.
The attacker needs a transition object; monitoring that object compresses the problem.
evidence: problem statement: intruder used the tender as a step
problem statement: avoid false alarms while intercepting before boarding
